critic_findings.finding: The new volatility-compression unit suite was thin and lacked an explicit passing-holdout/failing-train fold-gate negative control.
critic_findings.rationale: Finalizer added the train-before-holdout negative control and hardened the same fail-closed machinery against nonnumeric realized-volatility inputs.

critic_findings.finding: A fourth consecutive PCS daily-bar signal family on the same eight symbols may indicate loop familiarity rather than useful novelty.
critic_findings.rationale: Not a blocker for this wake: lagged realized-volatility state is a new observed-underlying evidence class absent from prior novelty keys, the DNA was predeclared without a grid, and the run closed rather than tuned the family. Future pre-archive off-hours routing now prefers a non-PCS-signal novelty class or DIMINISHING_RETURNS.

data_dependencies: A distinct New York RTH market date is required to advance observed TSLL option archive density from 1/3 to 2/3.
Provider-backed historical simulation, observed-cost calibration, and L1 claims remain blocked until at least 3/3 distinct market dates.
next: On the next distinct New York RTH market date, append one all-expiration TSLL observation snapshot and verify archive density advances from 1/3 to 2/3 without duplicate identical rows; no provider-backed historical simulation, observed-cost calibration, or L1 claim before 3/3.

useful_deltas.summary: A predeclared lagged hv_20/hv_60<=0.80 PCS state failed all 24 expanding-origin train gates across eight symbols under dual proxy costs and hard path-risk gates; the family is closed without tuning or registration.
Realized-volatility state parsing now fails closed on nonnumeric inputs and the new suite proves a passing holdout cannot rescue a failed train cost axis.
